methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $9M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Orbital Operations is a Seed-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Ben Schleuniger and Ross Doherty that has raised $8.8M to date. The broader sector comp universe includes 5 public equities (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers spanning various stages, though few operate at comparable stage and scale.

Of three valuation methods tested, only stage-progression proves applicable. This approach uses the $8.8M total raised as a proxy for pre-money value and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-Series A progression, yielding $22M. Peer-median analysis is not viable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparable data in the Space & Aerospace sector (fewer than 3 datapoints met narrow filter criteria across 11 same-sector peers examined). Public-comp anchoring cannot proceed because Orbital Operations' revenue is not on file; while the median P/S for the 5 public tickers is 25.8x, application requires disclosed target revenue.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $19M to $25M, with $22M as the median point. This range reflects the single applicable method and carries material uncertainty: the stage-progression multiplier is calibrated to typical rather than sector-specific progression rates, and absence of Seed-stage peer data limits cross-validation. Revenue disclosure or identification of additional private comparables at similar maturity would strengthen conviction in this range.
